mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
This step installs the Google Cloud SDK, which includes the gcloud command-line tool necessary for interacting with GKE.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

```

> Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
> Reload the shell to access gcloud commands.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.
commands: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the GKE cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step enables autoscaling and sets up monitoring and RBAC for your GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a

gcloud services enable monitoring.googleapis.com

kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(gcloud config get-value account)

```

> Enables cluster autoscaling.
> Enables Google Cloud Monitoring.
> Grants cluster admin permissions.
commands: gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(gcloud config get-value account)

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources like load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```
commands: gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your workloads and configurations. This step upgrades the GKE cluster to the latest version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --master --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

kubectl get nodes

```

> Upgrade the master node.
> Upgrade the worker nodes.
> Verify that all nodes are updated and in 'Ready' state.
commands: gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --master --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The user does not have the necessary permissions to perform the action.

**Fix:**
Ensure the user has the correct IAM roles assigned, such as 'Kubernetes Engine Admin'.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the nodes are still initializing or if there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl describe nodes' and ensure there are enough resources available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are insufficient quotas for the project.

**Fix:**
Check your Google Cloud quotas and increase them if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new GKE features such as Autopilot mode and enhanced workload identity.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy cluster autoscaler; use the new autoscaler instead.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that your workloads are compatible with the new autoscaler.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to the following when everything is working: 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
my-gke-cluster   Ready    control-plane   10m   v1.31.0'
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31
gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
kubectl create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(gcloud config get-value account)

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured with billing enabled.
